title: Quasi-3D Thermal Simulation of Integrated Circuit Systems in Packages
boundary_evidence: Package top exchanges heat convectively; the PCB exterior can be fixed at ambient or assigned a separate convective condition
p1e_support_scope: Explicit package-to-PCB path and independently specified exterior boundaries
limitation: Does not prescribe a transferable paired top/bottom h grid

title: Thermal Implications of Non-Uniform Power in BSPDN-Enabled 2.5D/3D Chiplet-based Systems-in-Package using Nanosheet Technology
boundary_evidence: Directly reports unequal top/bottom heat-transfer coefficients of 2500 and 200 W/(m2 K) at 25 C
p1e_support_scope: Strong asymmetric package-boundary anchor; top=2500 and bottom=200 endpoints
limitation: One package/cooling setup is not a probability distribution over h

title: A Simple Method for Thermal Characterization of Stacked Die Electronic Packages in Staggered Arrangement
venue: Journal of Microelectronics and Electronic Packaging
system: Five-die BGA stack on JEDEC PCB
boundary_evidence: Free convection uses 5 W/(m2 K) at PCB top and 2.5 W/(m2 K) at PCB bottom; mold convection is varied from free to forced-air values
p1e_support_scope: Direct top/bottom asymmetry; PCB path; free-to-forced convection and independent power configurations
limitation: Small package-power regime and much larger PCB than V6

title: Experimental and Numerical Study of Natural Convection for High Powered and Wire-Bonded QFN64b Electronic Device
venue: International Communications in Heat and Mass Transfer
boundary_evidence: Separate correlations for package top side bottom and PCB surfaces demonstrate surface-dependent natural convection
p1e_support_scope: Natural-convection asymmetry and separate board/package surface treatment
limitation: Local fitted surface coefficients include conjugate package/board effects and cannot be transplanted as a uniform package h

title: Forced and free convective heat transfer coefficients for a model printed circuit board channel geometry
boundary_evidence: Measures both free and forced convection; coefficient depends on channel geometry component height airflow and position
power_or_heat_flux_evidence: Heat flux up to 5000 W/m2 and air velocity up to 15 m/s
p1e_support_scope: Evidence that natural/forced cooling and chassis/channel placement require distinct boundary families
limitation: Channel-local coefficients are not a uniform top/bottom package coefficient

title: Numerical and experimental investigation on heat transfer of multi-heat sources mounted on an array of printed circuit boards in a rectangular case
system: Multiple PCB assemblies in an aluminum module
boundary_evidence: Experiments and CFD compare natural and 1-4 m/s forced convection; orientation and distance to the module wall change cooling
power_or_heat_flux_evidence: 4x4 sources at 0.5 W each per PCBA
p1e_support_scope: PCB-to-chassis path; natural/forced cooling; layout and enclosure effects
limitation: Conjugate CFD results should not be collapsed to one scalar h without calibration

title: 3-D conjugate heat transfer analysis of PLCC packages mounted in-line on a Printed Circuit Board
venue: International Communications in Heat and Mass Transfer
system: PLCC packages on PCB in forced airflow
boundary_evidence: Conjugate package/board cooling changes with air velocity power and package spacing
power_or_heat_flux_evidence: Power and spacing are varied experimentally/numerically
p1e_support_scope: Forced-air package/PCB evidence and geometry dependence
limitation: Does not provide a universal uniform h grid for the V6 stack

title: 3D-ICE Fast Compact Transient Thermal Modeling for 3D ICs with Inter-tier Liquid Cooling
boundary_evidence: Cooling is represented by coolant flow inlet/outlet and solid-fluid coupling rather than a scalar film coefficient
limitation: A flow-defined liquid boundary cannot justify a uniform Robin h

title: MASS-HBM architecture reference scope restriction
boundary_evidence: Retained only as qualitative evidence that HBM architecture and high-power liquid cooling are relevant scenarios
p1e_support_scope: Architecture relevance and high-power/liquid-cooling scenario only
limitation: No bibliographic or experimental uniform-h value is inferred; it must not source any P1e scalar h endpoint
